title: crepis occidentalis var. pumila
intro: Crepis occidentalis var. pumila, also known as dwarf western hawk's beard or small western hawk's beard, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. Crepis occidentalis var. pumila is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
